groups.name: Strategic Thinking & Decision-Making
groups.neuroscience: Executive function, working memory, planning, cognitive flexibility, value-based decisions, abstract reasoning, metacognition
groups.offensive: Formulating attack chains, sequencing exploitation phases, planning lateral movement
groups.defensive: Designing defense-in-depth strategies, incident response planning, threat modeling
groups.operator_frame: The mission command center — like a general planning operations or a surgeon sequencing a complex procedure

groups.name: Procedural Fluency & Muscle Memory
groups.neuroscience: Motor planning, procedural memory, action sequencing, sensorimotor integration, fine/gross motor control
groups.offensive: Operationalizing exploit techniques into repeatable workflows, command-line fluency
groups.defensive: Rapid firewall rule deployment, scripted response procedures, forensic tool chains
groups.operator_frame: Knowledge translated into automatic action — like a pilot's stick-and-throttle instincts or a surgeon's trained hands

groups.name: Technical Comprehension & Language Processing
groups.neuroscience: Syntax parsing, semantic understanding, speech comprehension, reading, complex sequence processing, auditory processing
groups.offensive: Parsing CVE descriptions, understanding exploit code, processing protocol specifications
groups.defensive: Reading threat intel advisories, understanding SIEM alert logic, interpreting log syntax
groups.operator_frame: Decoding complex technical briefings under pressure — like a pilot parsing rapid ATC instructions

groups.name: Visual Processing & Pattern Detection
groups.neuroscience: Visual feature detection, expert pattern recognition, anomaly detection, color/form processing, visual memory, face/object recognition
groups.offensive: Spotting misconfigurations in scan output, recognizing vulnerable patterns in code
groups.defensive: Detecting anomalies in log streams, recognizing attack signatures, PCAP analysis
groups.operator_frame: A fighter pilot's ability to spot a threat in a cluttered radar display — trained eyes that see what others miss

groups.name: Situational Awareness & Focus
groups.neuroscience: Spatial reasoning, sustained/directed attention, numerical cognition, mental model maintenance
groups.offensive: Maintaining awareness of the full attack surface, tracking pivoted sessions and credentials
groups.defensive: Monitoring network topology, tracking incident scope, correlating multi-source alerts
groups.operator_frame: Maintaining the big picture while executing the details — the pilot's instrument scan pattern that keeps everything in view

groups.name: Adaptive Response & Error Recovery
groups.neuroscience: Error detection, conflict monitoring, strategy switching, cognitive control, motivation, pain/frustration processing
groups.offensive: Recognizing when an exploit path fails, pivoting to alternative vectors mid-operation
groups.defensive: Distinguishing false positives from real threats, adjusting detection rules on the fly
groups.operator_frame: A surgeon recognizing complications and adapting the procedure in real-time — sharp reflexes in the cognitive domain

groups.name: Memory Encoding & Knowledge Retention
groups.neuroscience: Episodic memory encoding, contextual/scene memory, spatial navigation memory, semantic memory
groups.offensive: Encoding new exploitation techniques, building mental maps of target environments
groups.defensive: Retaining indicator patterns, building mental models of attacker behavior over time
groups.operator_frame: The difference between cramming for an exam and actually retaining field knowledge — building the expertise that sticks

groups.name: Knowledge Synthesis & Transfer
groups.neuroscience: Analogical reasoning, cross-modal integration, mathematical reasoning, phonological processing, applying abstract principles to new contexts
groups.offensive: Applying techniques from one environment to a completely different target
groups.defensive: Recognizing that a novel attack shares the pattern of known TTPs, cross-domain correlation
groups.operator_frame: Applying training from one theater of operations to an entirely different one — the hallmark of elite operators who can improvise in novel situations

groups.name: Rapid Threat Assessment & Intuition
groups.neuroscience: Interoception, risk evaluation, salience detection, time-pressure decision-making, emotional awareness
groups.offensive: Snap judgments about whether a target is honeypot or real, triaging opportunities
groups.defensive: Rapid incident triage, deciding escalation priority under extreme time pressure
groups.operator_frame: The split-second 'something's wrong' instinct that separates experts from novices — gut-level threat detection honed by experience

groups.name: Deep Internalization & Reflective Processing
groups.neuroscience: Self-referential processing, episodic memory retrieval, mental simulation, reflective thinking, rest-state consolidation, visuospatial imagery
groups.offensive: Internalizing techniques so deeply they become second nature, mental rehearsal of operations
groups.defensive: Post-incident reflection, lessons-learned integration, converting experience into lasting intuition
groups.operator_frame: The difference between knowing a procedure and truly owning it — converting classroom training into combat-ready instinct
